title: noronhia urceolata
intro: Noronhia urceolata, also known as the 'Devil's back bone' plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Olacaceae, native to Madagascar. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, among others. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Noronhia urceolata are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tonics, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
